terms.definition: ML paradigm that learns f: X → Y from labeled training pairs (x, y), minimizing loss between predictions ŷ and true labels. Canonical definition in this topic.
Dataset where each input x has an associated known target y used as the training signal.
The output y the model should predict — a class, category, or numeric value provided during training.
Labeled examples used to fit model parameters θ by minimizing loss L(ŷ, y).
Feedback from known correct outputs y that guides learning, as opposed to discovering structure without labels.
Synonym emphasizing that explicit per-example labels distinguish supervised learning from unsupervised approaches.
